summary.intent: Implement tasks #297, #298, #299 in the ars-ui Rust component library: the framework-agnostic core of 8 color components (ColorSwatch, ColorField, ColorArea, ColorSlider, ColorWheel, AngleSlider, ColorSwatchPicker) plus a shared ColorValue foundation, delivered as one combined PR.
summary.outcome: All 8 components implemented and passing CI (PR #698), with 33 Codex review findings addressed across 7 rounds; all 22 CI checks green including a post-hoc coverage fix. Awaiting Codex final 👍 and user go-ahead before merge.

repo: core_maths is the workspace's no_std float tool; fully-qualified form CoreFloat::method(x) is required to match ars-i18n convention; CoreFloat lacks min/max so private fmin/fmax helpers are needed.

MessageFn uses hand-written From<F> impls per distinct callback signature — there is no blanket impl; new color signatures (Fn(&ColorNameParts,&Locale), Fn(ColorChannel,&Locale), Fn(ColorChannel,f64,&Locale), Fn(&str,&str,&Locale), Fn(&str,&str,&str,&Locale)) had to be added to message_fn.rs.

Adapter-driven drag pattern: the adapter owns pointer capture and fires DragMove/DragEnd events; core fires a typed Effect::ChangeEnd on DragEnd. The spec described non-existent use_platform_effects()/track_pointer_drag() APIs that don't exist in ars-core.

Bindable<T>: get() returns the controlled prop, set() updates internal, pending() (added this session) returns internal — pending() is necessary for correct display during a drag on a controlled component.

ColorValue::new wraps hue to [0,360) as a documented invariant. Hue endpoints (slider 360°, wheel SetToMax) must track an unwrapped slider_value/hue_value separately; the stored color is always normalized.
trim_start_matches('#') strips ALL leading # chars (##3366ff → 3366ff); use strip_prefix('#').unwrap_or(hex) to strip at most one.
Part::Marker { value: f64 } cannot use #[derive(ComponentPart)] because f64 is not Eq/Hash; requires a manual impl using to_bits() for hashing/equality.
ars-core is built --no-default-features (no_std) in CI; the project has no bare-metal target but follows no_std convention for wasm compatibility.

The snapshot-count xci-fast gate requires at least one insta snapshot per declared state variant; AngleSlider's three states (Idle/Dragging/Focused) initially had only two snapshots and failed this gate.
Spec code blocks are compiled by the spec-compile-snippets gate; cross-block symbol references require a no_check fence annotation to avoid compilation errors.

code.finding: from_hex byte-indexed into &hex[0..2] without an ASCII check, panicking on non-ASCII inputs like #ああ (6 bytes, non-char boundaries). Fixed with an is_ascii() early-return guard.

parse_color_string accepted hsl(NaN,...) and hsl(inf,...) because parse::<f64>() succeeds for those literals. Fixed by routing all numeric parses through a parse_finite_f64 helper that returns None for non-finite values.

format_color_string for Hsb emitted hsb(h,s%,b%) without alpha, so a commit+reparse on a translucent color silently set alpha=1.0. Fixed by adding hsba() emission and a corresponding parse_hsb_args branch.

format_value displayed percentage channels as 0–100 but commit_input parsed the raw input and clamped to [0,1], so typing '50' committed 1.0 (fully saturated) instead of 0.5. Fixed with /100 scaling in commit and *100 in format.

The IME CompositionUpdate/Change branch ran before the readonly/disabled guard, so a readonly field mid-composition still had its input_text replaced. Fixed by guarding the IME arm on ctx.readonly || ctx.disabled.

Props::readonly only set aria-readonly; the machine still accepted Change events and blur-committed input. Fixed with HtmlAttr::ReadOnly on the native input and an early-return in the Change and blur-commit arms when ctx.readonly.

In spinbutton (channel) mode, aria-valuenow/min/max exposed the 0–1 internal range while the visible input and commit used 0–100 for percentage channels. Fixed by scaling ARIA values by 100 via channel_is_percentage().

An invalid commit left ctx.value at the old color with ctx.invalid=true, but the hidden form input still emitted the old hex value, so form submission sent a stale color. Fixed by omitting the hidden input value when ctx.invalid.

has_description flag had no setter event, making the Description part's aria-describedby permanently unreachable (dead branch). Added Event::SetHasDescription(bool) mirroring the field component pattern; also found by coverage analysis.

The disabled guard rejected Blur events, leaving the machine stuck in Focused with focused/focus_visible flags set when disabled mid-focus. Fixed by allowing Blur (and is_prop_sync events) through the guard.

focused_index was not cleared on Blur, so item_attrs kept data-ars-focused on the old item while the root was in Idle state. Fixed by gating the focused styling on State::Focused instead of focused_index.

In Idle state all items had tabindex=-1 (roving tabindex without an entry point), making it impossible to tab into the picker. Fixed with tabbable_index() that gives the selected item (or first) tabindex=0 when idle.

Controlled-mode on_change_end read ctx.value.get() (the stale controlled prop), not the just-dragged value. Fixed by adding Bindable::pending() and passing pending() into the ChangeEnd effect; also introduced slider_value for unwrapped hue tracking.

Controlled drag showed mismatched aria-valuetext (color name from get() = old controlled color) while thumb position tracked slider_value (pending). Fixed by deriving the display color from pending() in thumb_attrs, track_attrs, and hidden_input.

set_x_value and set_y_value each read ctx.value.get() as the base color, so IncrementX followed by IncrementY used the stale controlled color for the second update, discarding the first axis change. Fixed by basing both helpers on pending().

aria-valuetext showed raw 0–1 channel values multiplied by 100, so a Hue x-axis rendered '18000%'. Fixed with a channel-aware format_axis_reading() helper that formats hue as degrees, RGB as integers, and fractional channels as percentages.
Decrement with large_step > 360 produced a negative hue via the % operator (e.g. step=720 from hue=10 → -710). Fixed with rem_euclid(360.0).

SetToMax wrote hue:360 directly into ColorValue, bypassing new() normalization and violating the [0,360) invariant. Fixed with hue_value tracking (same approach as color_slider) so the stored color is always normalized while ARIA reports 360.
thumb_attrs set aria-labelledby to a *-label id but ColorWheel has no Label part, creating a dangling IDREF. Fixed by dropping aria-labelledby (aria-label was already set).
SetProps updated min/max but did not re-clamp ctx.value, leaving an out-of-range value in aria-valuenow and the hidden input. Fixed by re-clamping to [new_min, new_max] in the SetProps arm.

workflow: Mandatory post-implementation spec audit (spec validate + snippet compile) catches both behavioral bugs and documentation drift systematically; running it before committing avoids separate spec-sync PRs.
A dedicated spec-sync subagent is effective for mechanical mirroring of implementation changes across multiple spec files — saves main-context tokens while keeping specs in sync reliably.

CI coverage uses --workspace, which lifts ars-components by ~1.1% over -p-only measurement. Always replicate the exact CI command (cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch --workspace) before declaring coverage fixed.
Both-arms tests for one-armed if flag conditionals are required to clear the branch-coverage threshold; the coverage fix commit should be driven by annotated lcov output, not line coverage alone.
When Codex review is rate-limited, stop polling (pointless) and surface the rate-limit message explicitly so the user can decide whether to wait or proceed.

For cyclic/linear numeric ranges where the underlying type normalizes values (e.g. hue [0,360)), keep an unwrapped tracking field (slider_value, hue_value, x_value, y_value) as the source of truth for ARIA and position; derive the stored color from it.
Disabled guards in drag machines must not block DragEnd — disabling mid-drag must still let the drag terminate cleanly, or the machine gets wedged in Dragging state.
The zsh status variable is read-only; background poll scripts must use a different variable name (e.g. cov_state) to avoid silent crashes.

friction: Codex was rate-limited on the first two triggers ('You have reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews'), blocking the review loop for hours with no actionable path forward.
Coverage CI failed after initial implementation: ars-components branch 89.7% was below the 90% threshold; required a separate coverage-fix commit after the main implementation.
Background CI watcher scripts crashed due to zsh's read-only 'status' variable — silent failure that looked like a CI pass until the output file was read.
Bash safety classifier was temporarily unavailable during pass-6, blocking the reply/resolve/re-trigger step and requiring a manual retry.
Spec code blocks described non-existent APIs (use_platform_effects(), track_pointer_drag()) requiring full spec reconciliation across 6 files before the implementation could be considered correct.
core_maths::CoreFloat lacks min/max despite the docs implying otherwise — required adding private fmin/fmax helpers after discovering the gap at build time.

A pre-existing pagination/mod.rs clippy lint (unnecessary_option_map_or_else) was surfaced by stable 1.96 toolchain upgrade and blocked xci-fast, requiring an unplanned 1-line fix to an unrelated file.

7 rounds of Codex review totaling 33 findings — significantly higher churn than expected; several findings were cross-cutting (controlled-mode display, disabled hidden inputs, hue endpoint tracking) affecting 4–6 components each.
Spec agent output could not be verified via SendMessage mid-turn — required scheduling a wakeup and having the user manually continue the loop after each spec-sync round.
open_items: Codex final 👍 approval pending (watcher running for pass 8); no merge without user go-ahead + Codex approval.
Issues #297/#298/#299 auto-close only on merge — not yet closed.
Framework adapter implementation for all 8 color components (Leptos/Dioxus) is explicitly out of scope per the issues and deferred to a future task.

ColorValue type could be promoted from color-picker.md §Types to spec/shared/color-types.md for discoverability — deferred as it touches the manifest and 8 spec frontmatters without affecting implementation correctness.
color_area aria-valuetext includes the English color name via color_name_en() but lacks i18n parity with color_slider (which uses the MessageFn value_text); could be unified in a follow-up.
Proptest suites for new components are marked #[ignore] (nightly-only) and not exercised in the normal CI fast path — coverage and edge cases depend on periodic nightly runs.
